title: Marka Ragnos
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Marka}} {{Character |type=Sith |image=[[File:Markaragnosheadshot.jpg]] |name=Marka Ragnos |homeworld= |birth=Prior to [[5100 BBY/Legends|5100 BBY]]''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' |death=[[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 2]] |species=[[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]]-[[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Hybrid/Legends|hybrid]]''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Black]]''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]''[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 6|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 6]] or Gold''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Shadow of Revan]]'' |skin=Obsidian''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Shadow of Revan]]'' or Pale[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 6|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 6]] |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] *[[Disciples of Ragnos]] |masters= |apprentices=*[[Tenebrae]]''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]'' *[[Tavion Axmis]] }} {{Quote|The chains of the grave cannot hold a Dark Lord of the Sith…|Marka Ragnos|}} '''Marka Ragnos''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Hybrid/Legends|half-breed]] [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] who lived during the time of the first [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], born before [[5100 BBY/Legends|5100 BBY]], and [[Duel between Marka Ragnos and Simus|dueled]] [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Simus]] for the title of Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] and control of the Sith Empire. Ragnos defeated [[Simus]], who was [[Sai cha/Legends|beheaded]] in the battle with the other aspiring Dark Lord. Ragnos lived for over a hundred years, growing in power, pitting his adversaries against each other and ensuring that there were no threats to his throne. Following his death shortly before the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]], two powerful Sith Lords, [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] and [[Ludo Kressh]], fought each other to occupy his throne; Ragnos' spirit appeared before them and proclaimed that only the most worthy should succeed him. Ragnos lived on as a [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]] after his death, trapped within the confines of [[Tomb of Marka Ragnos|his tomb]] in the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. [[3997 BBY|A thousand years after the Dark Lord's death]], [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] and [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] accidentally summoned Ragnos from his grave through the use of [[Sith talisman/Legends|Sith amulets]]. Ragnos crowned Kun as the new Dark Lord, with Qel-Droma as Kun's apprentice, hoping to resurrect the lost empire of 1000 years prior. [[14 ABY/Legends|Thousands of years after the Sith Empire]], during the [[New Republic era]], the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Tavion Axmis]] attempted to bring Marka Ragnos back from the depths from [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], using an old Sith artifact, known as the [[Scepter of Ragnos]] in order to siphon the Force from many Force Nexuses all spread across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], empowering the [[Disciples of Ragnos]]. Tavion's goal to resurrect Ragnos almost succeeded, but her operation was overthrown by the Jedi [[Jaden Korr]], defeating Ragnos' spirit, and enclosing him in his tomb once again, where he was dragged back in the depths of the Force. ==Biography== ===Dark Lord of the Sith=== {{Quote|Once we were mighty Jedi of the Republic, brothers in the Force. But the Great Schism between the dark side and the light turned Jedi against Jedi. Our ancestors were defeated, driven off…cast out!|Marka Ragnos describes the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]]|}} [[File:Markaragnos.jpg|thumb|left|The shade of Marka Ragnos]] A [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]]-[[Human/Legends|Human]] hybrid, Marka Ragnos was a direct descendant from a [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] who was [[Exiles|exiled]] from the Republic after the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]], who lived in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]. Feared, obeyed, and admired among the Sith,''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' Ragnos was considered to have been the greatest [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] of his age, and was born centuries before 5000 BBY. When the Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] died, Ragnos and another powerful Sith, [[Simus]], rose up to take the mantle. A formidable warrior, [[Duel between Marka Ragnos and Simus|Ragnos dueled and defeated Simus]] in combat[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 3]] at some point from [[5200 BBY]] to [[5100 BBY/Legends|5100 BBY]], proving himself worthy of the mantle of Dark Lord of the Sith. After gaining the mantle of a Dark Lord, he led ruthless campaigns against his Sith enemies, pitting his adversaries against each other and ensuring that there were no threats to his throne. His iron-fisted rule lasted over a century.''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' During those times Ragnos likely composed [[Epistle of Marka Ragnos|his own epistle]],[[Knights of the Old Republic 25|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 25]] created a [[Scepter of Ragnos|Scepter]] capable of siphoning [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] from many different places, which could be used to empower non-Force users, and designed a pair of [[Marka Ragnos' gauntlets|gauntlets]] capable of augmenting the strength of the Force in its user. In [[5100 BBY/Legends|5100 BBY]], Ragnos, as Dark Lord of the Sith and the ruler of the [[Sith Council]] and [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], was petitioned by a thirteen-year-old boy named [[Tenebrae]]. The youth had come before Marka Ragnos to ask for training in the Sith arts and for recognition as the ruler of his father's domain, the planet [[Nathema|Medriaas]]. Although he declined to take him as an apprentice, Lord Ragnos was impressed by Tenebrae's power and ambition, and named him the ruler of Medriaas, bestowing on him the rank of [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] and the name Vitiate.''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]'' After years of his rule, Marka Ragnos [[Death/Legends|passed away]] in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]. His death was marked as the end of a golden age of the Sith Empire, despite its reluctance to continue expansion. ===Legacy=== {{Quote|All hail the memory of Marka Ragnos! His legacy will live for centuries among the Sith people and their rulers!|Ludo Kressh|}} [[File:LudoVsSadow.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Naga Sadow duels Ludo Kressh for the mantle of Dark Lord, while Ragnos oversees them.]] Ragnos' legacy lived after his death. The [[Ragnos Lakes]], on [[Kesh/Legends|Kesh]], for instance, were named after him.''[[Lost Tribe of the Sith: Paragon]]'' During [[Funeral of Marka Ragnos|his funeral]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], two Sith Lords, [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] and [[Ludo Kressh]], emerged as favorites to succeed Ragnos as the next Dark Lord of the Sith and the two engaged in a duel of [[Sith sword]]s, after a disagreement as to who would better serve the Empire as Dark Lord. Ragnos' spirit appeared before them speaking of a great past and an uncertain future for the Sith Empire, proclaiming that only the most worthy would succeed him. After the duel, Sadow was elected as the new Dark Lord of the Sith. After the inconvenient arrival of [[Gav Daragon|Gav]] and [[Jori Daragon]], Sadow had an excuse to invade the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]], claiming that those Galactic Explorers would try to bring Republic reinforcements in an effort to invade the Sith Empire soon enough. Vitiate did not join in the struggle for succession after Ragnos' death, nor was he involved in the conflict between Sadow and Kressh,[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 1]] or the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]].''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire]]'' Instead, he decided to stay hidden, until the right time arrived. After Sadow's election as the new Dark Lord of the Sith, further events led to a [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|major war]] with the Galactic Republic. Ultimately, because the schism was never healed, it spelled the end of their interstellar empire.''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' Ragnos lived long after his death as a [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]], trapped within the confines of his own tomb in the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]] on Korriban. [[File:Exarulicsithlords_b.jpg|thumb|left|230px|Marka Ragnos proclaiming Exar Kun and Ulic Qel-Droma as Lords of the Sith]] In [[3997 BBY]], about one millennium after his death, the spirits of Ragnos and several [[Unidentified Sith group|other Dark Lords]] were summoned from the grave by [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] and [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] with the help of a pair of [[Sith talisman/Legends|Sith amulets]] as part of an [[Dark Lord prophecy|ancient prophecy]]. Awakened once again, he crowned Kun as the new Dark Lord of the Sith, his initiation into the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] complete—with a single touch, Ragnos burned a [[Mark of Dark Lord of the Sith|mark of a Dark Lord]]''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' into the center of Kun's forehead, and Qel-Droma with [[Mark of Shadow Hand|that]] of a [[Shadow Hand]], whom Ragnos said would become Kun's first and foremost apprentice, all in an attempt to restore the lost empire of a thousand years prior.[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 6|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 6]] The [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] devastated [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] once again, but the Sith Empire was not restored.''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War]]'' At some point prior to [[3693 BBY|3963 BBY]], [[Celeste Morne]] destroyed the last known copy of [[Epistle of Marka Ragnos|Ragnos' epistle]]. {{GameLSmechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}} A thousand years after his physical death, during the [[Jedi Civil War]], [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and his companions [[Skirmish on Korriban|visited]] the tomb of Marka Ragnos, during their search for the [[Star Forge]]. Revan had to enter the tomb and retrieve an old Sith artifact in order to impress [[Uthar Wynn]] so that Wynn would grant him access to Naga Sadow's tomb, where a Star Map was located. Revan had to make his way into the [[Tomb of Marka Ragnos|Ragnos' tomb]], which was protected by a [[Mark VII experimental prototype assassin droid|rogue assassin droid]]. After battling the droid forces inside the tomb, Revan and his companions encountered the [[Mark VII experimental prototype assassin droid|assassin droid]]. After conversing with the droid, he said that he was originally programmed to kill [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], but his Sith programmers made his cognitive systems more independent than they desired. Since then, the droid kept himself in isolation within the confines of Ragnos' tomb, building droids to aid in his protection. After that, the droid asked Revan for assistance in changing his programming, because, although the droid had developed the desire to not to kill, his programming was often at odds with his desires. Then, the droid offered Revan assistance to help him change his programming. After the reprogramming of the droid was successful, he offered Revan several of his redundant systems, so he could use them on other droids, and then the droid stated that he could escape from the planet, wishing well to Revan. Before exiting the tomb, Revan also found a [[Marka Ragnos' gauntlets|pair of gauntlets]] that once belonged to Marka Ragnos. After that, Revan went out of the tomb, to [[Star Map|gain prestige]] with Uthar Wynn. {{Endgame}} In [[4980 BBY]],''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]'' Vitiate, now known as the Sith Emperor, [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted the Sith Empire]]. The Sith Emperor ignited a [[Great Galactic War|new war]] against the Galactic Republic 1,319 years after Ragnos's death.''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' During the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]], [[Jedi historian]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Gnost-Dural]] speculated that Ragnos's support for Exar Kun meant that Ragnos didn't support the Sith Emperor.{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 10: The Exar Kun War|int=Timeline 10: The Exar Kun War}} By the time of the [[Galactic War]], the ghost of Ragnos roamed the cave known as [[Architect's Hollow]], on the [[Yavin 4/Legends|fourth]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin Prime]].''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Shadow of Revan]]'' [[File:Ragnos_Book_of_Sith.jpg|thumb|right|230px|Ragnos appears to Plagueis in a vision on Korriban.]] At some point in his life, [[Darth Plagueis/Legends|Darth Plagueis]] traveled to the Valley of the Dark Lords in Korriban. While there, he found no evidence that the spirits of Korriban were real, believing that those were mere diversions created for the credulous, but as Plagueis boarded his ship, he beheld a vision of Marka Ragnos. The apparition challenged Plagueis' claim to the Sith title, and railed against his plan to dismantle the traditions of Korriban. The vision, however, did not answer Plagueis' questions. Instead, Ragnos snarled and disappeared in a whirl of smoke. After the experience, Plagueis wrote that it was possible that the entire episode simply played out in his mind. Those texts were later acquired by [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], Plagueis' former apprentice, and were included in a book he called, "[[Book of Sith]]."''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]'' ===Resurrection attempt=== {{Quote|Ragnos? What can a dead Sith Lord give you?''"
"''Everything I have ever wanted.|[[Kyle Katarn]] and [[Tavion Axmis]]|}} [[File:JKAlevelshot_kor2.png|thumb|left|The entrance to Marka Ragnos' tomb at the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]] in [[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]]]] Thousands of years after his actual death, during the [[New Republic era]] in [[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]], Ragnos' spirit was intended to be resurrected from beyond the grave, this time summoned by a Sith cult known as the [[Disciples of Ragnos]] whose leader was [[Tavion Axmis]], [[Desann]]'s student. With the help of an ancient scepter once used by Ragnos to drain residual Force energy, Tavion and [[Alora (Dark Jedi)|her apprentice]] siphoned the Force from many Force Nexuses all spread around the galaxy and used it to empower [[Sith cultist/Legends|their followers]]. Tavion plotted to use the collected energy to resurrect Ragnos. After a hard campaign tracking the Disciples of Ragnos across the galaxy, the [[New Jedi Order]] managed to track Tavion on Korriban. Jedi [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Kyle Katarn]] were in charge of the campaign on Korriban, dispatching every available Jedi from the temple on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], among them, the Jedi [[Jaden Korr]], who participated on almost every mission against the Disciples of Ragnos. During the [[Battle of Korriban (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|Battle of Korriban]], Jaden Korr managed to enter Ragnos' tomb, intending to stop Axmis. He was nearly too late, as she managed to resurrect Ragnos by allowing his spirit to possess her body, but Ragnos himself was nevertheless defeated by Korr. Ragnos' ghost was forced back into his sarcophagus, screaming out a dark promise that he would return. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|A mere Jedi child will not undo my return.|Marka Ragnos to [[Jaden Korr]]|audio=29mar002.flac|}} [[File:Markaragnos23.jpg|thumb|right|Marka Ragnos]] Marka Ragnos was a respected, feared and obeyed Dark Lord of the Sith, considered wise and powerful. In order to ensure his seat as a Dark Lord of the Sith, Ragnos conducted ruthless campaigns against his Sith enemies, marking the beginning of a Golden Age for the Sith. After his death, Naga Sadow and Ludo Kressh fought to decide who would succeed him, and Ragnos' Force ghost made himself present before them, claiming that only the most worthy would be his successor. Ragnos' importance among the Sith was so high that he was summoned back from death twice, first by Exar Kun and Ulic Qel-Droma with the help of talismans. Ragnos chose Exar Kun over Ulic, crowning him as the next Dark Lord, while he assigned Qel-Droma as his apprentice. Later, thousands of years after his death, during the [[14 ABY/Legends|New Republic era]], Dark Jedi Tavion Axmis believed that if she resurrected Marka Ragnos, she would be able to bring back a new Golden Age for the Sith. When he was defeated by the Jedi [[Jaden Korr]], he angrily exclaimed that he would return. ==Powers and abilities== {{Quote|It matters not who I am. My power is all that concerns you. You have both found the amulets…and each other…now the power of these amulets is joined…and I have come.|Marka Ragnos|}} [[File:Scepterofragnos.jpg|thumb|left|325px|Marka Ragnos' scepter]] Marka Ragnos was powerful, both physically and in the use of the Force, and was considered to be among the most powerful Sith and Force-users in Sith and Jedi history, leading the Sith Empire to a Golden Age of their existence, conducting brutal and ruthless campaigns against his Sith enemies. Ragnos had such knowledge about the Force that even during his funeral, he was able to show himself to Naga Sadow and Ludo Kressh as a spirit, orchestrating their duel, thus deciding the future of the Sith Empire. He also knew how to keep the essence of his body alive, 5000 years after his death, and was proficient in the use of [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]]. Ragnos' connection with the Force was strong enough to possess [[Tavion Axmis]] in order to fight against [[Jaden Korr]]. Ragnos didn't always wield a lightsaber, the common weapon of the Sith Lords''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]'' and the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]];[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']] instead, he mostly wielded a Sith sword, consisting of a metal blade enhanced by Sith sorcery, a weapon of the primitive Sith race, which he could hide inside his scepter. It is possible only he knew of the sword's hiding place, as Tavion Axmis did not draw it until she was possessed by Ragnos. His skills with his customized Sith sword were remarkable in his [[Battle of Korriban (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|duel]] against Jaden Korr inside of [[Tomb of Marka Ragnos|his own tomb]], as he could skillfully deflect the attacks Korr provided against him, and at the same time, he could unleash the power of the Sith magic with which his sword was imbued. Ragnos also owned a pair of dark gauntlets which increased his skill with a melee weapon or a lightsaber. According to [[Lokar]], he could also focus the dark side to make himself nearly unwoundable in battle. ==Equipment== Marka Ragnos had a mystical Scepter, which had the ability to siphon and store dark-side energy, and it could also release the power of the Force on ordinary beings to make them Force-sensitive. {{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}} [[File:Marka_ragnos_guantlets.jpg|thumb|right|Marka Ragnos' gauntlets]] He also made a pair of gauntlets which he used to increase his affinity with the dark side of the Force. After his death, during the Jedi Civil War when Revan searched for the Star Maps, he retrieved the gauntlets that once belonged to Ragnos inside of his tomb. {{Endgame}} ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Tojdls6cover.jpg|thumb|left|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith 6: Jedi Assault'' was Marka Ragnos' first appearance.]] {{Quote|Through the whole process, there was only one that came back from Lucas with regards to the designs I had initially sketched and it was the Sarcophagus of Marka Ragnos…|[[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]] regarding his designs used in [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']]|[[Wookieepedia:Interview/Dario Carrasco, Jr.|Interview with Dario Carrasco, Jr.]]}} Marka Ragnos' first appearance was in the ''Star Wars'' comic [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 6|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 6]], written by [[Tom Veitch]] and [[Kevin J. Anderson]]. It was published on [[March 14]], [[1995]]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54576/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_-_dark_lords_of_the_sith_1994_6|text=Star Wars: Tales Of The Jedi - Dark Lords Of The Sith (1994) #6}} {{C|First printing}} and set in [[3997 BBY]], 1004 years after his death, making his first appearance as a Force ghost summoned by Exar Kun and Ulic Qel-Droma. There, he's surrounded by ships flying as if in combat and says, "In my time, even as [[Post–Great Hyperspace War counterinvasion|the Galactic Republic battles us to extinction]]." Furthermore, the comic's narration describes him as "the reigning Dark Lord of the Sith" during "a time when the Sith people were being driven to extinction by the Jedi Knights and the armies of the Galactic Republic." However, [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 2]] begins with Marka Ragnos having died before the Galactic Republic and the Sith Empire discover each other. While a figure of importance in Sith history, Marka Ragnos has never appeared in a story in which he was alive, much like another influential Sith Lord, [[Freedon Nadd]].[[Tales of the Jedi 2|''Tales of the Jedi'' 2]]''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' In addition to his appearances in comics, Ragnos has also appeared in the ''Star Wars'' games ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' as a mummified corpse and ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]'', voiced by [[Peter Lurie]].{{IMDb|url=character/ch0089806|text=Marka Ragnos (Character)}} In the latter game, Ragnos has a leading role, as the game centers on him. Marka Ragnos' first name was misspelled as "Marko" in the [[Wikipedia:Xbox|Xbox]] version of ''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic''. During the same game, when Revan travels to Korriban, the player has the choice to enter Marka Ragnos's tomb and to either aid or destroy the [[Mark VII experimental prototype assassin droid|rogue droid]] inside of Ragnos' tomb. Also, in the same level, the player has the option to search for a pair of gauntlets that once belonged to Ragnos, in order to amplify the player's affinity with the dark side of the Force during gameplay. During the creation of Ragnos' funeral as part of the [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi'' project]], [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]] initially had some problems with [[Lucasfilm]] regarding with his concepts of Ragnos' coffin, because his designs resembled too closely an [[Wikipedia:Egypt|Egyptian]] sarcophagus; however, it was approved after he submitted a modified design. Ragnos received a brief entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|soundcat=Audio files of Marka Ragnos|imagecat=Images of Marka Ragnos}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 2]] {{Ghost}} {{1stID|Marka Ragnos}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith]] |years=by [[5100 BBY/Legends|5100 BBY]] – [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]] |before=Eventually [[Tulak Hord]] |before-years= |after=[[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] |after-years=5000 BBY''[[Star
